design_philosophy.core_loop: Explore overworld and dungeons → defeat enemies in real-time combat → collect rings and equipment → progress to next area → face boss battles
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Real-time action combat vs. RPG progression; managing limited resources (health, magic) while exploring dangerous dungeons
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Unnamed prince reclaiming his kingdom and destiny by collecting the scattered rings of good, aided by his guardian Blade's legacy
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Hybrid gameplay architecture with four distinct play styles (top-down exploration, side-view boss fights, town interaction, overworld navigation); deliberate shift away from turn-based combat toward arcade-style action as a statement against Phantasy Star II's formula

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Player can obtain more powerful swords and magic spells from NPCs in towns, allowing resource-based difficulty mitigation

progression.upgrade_system: Equipment-based progression through acquisition of more powerful swords and magic spells obtained via NPC transactions in towns
progression.unlock_mechanism: Ring collection gates progression; 8 rings of good must be gathered to restore balance to the realm

world_building_techniques: NPCs in towns reveal story through dialogue interactions
Ring collection narrative framework (8 rings scattered by antagonist)
Character backstory: prince orphaned at birth, raised by knight Blade in village of Wyclif
Antagonist motivation: King Tsarkon corrupted by rings of evil, invaded Excalabria, petrified princess

boss_design.philosophy: Boss encounters use distinct side-on perspective to differentiate from standard exploration gameplay; separate visual presentation emphasizes encounter importance

faction_indicators: Kingdom of Excalabria (protagonist's realm, invaded and conquered)
Kingdom of Cartahena (antagonist's realm, corrupted by evil rings)
danger_signals: Castle collapse and royal family dispersal
environmental_storytelling: Village of Wyclif (refuge and hiding place for prince)
Scattered ring locations as markers of Tsarkon's control
